title: Food & Drinks: Ordering with Confidence
objectives: Order food and drinks, ask for the bill, and describe simple preferences.
Understand Carlos in short guided exchanges.
Use the lesson language in one realistic conversation.
objective: Order food and drinks, ask for the bill, and describe simple preferences.

grammar.explanation: Use con for with and sin for without. These tiny words are powerful in restaurants.
grammar.practicePrompt: Say one original sentence using today?s pattern with Carlos.

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.english: I would like a coffee, please.

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.english: What do you want to drink?

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.english: Good afternoon. I would like a table.

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.english: Of course, a table for two.

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.english: What do you want to eat?

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.english: Can you bring me bread, please?

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.english: Yes, I like it a lot.

culture.title: Food & Drinks in Real Life
culture.text: Meal times vary by country. Dinner may be later in Spain, while lunch is often the main meal in many Latin American families.

cultureNotes: Meal times vary by country. Dinner may be later in Spain, while lunch is often the main meal in many Latin American families.

nativeSpeech.title: How Carlos would say it naturally
notes: ?La cuenta, por favor? is the classic bill phrase.
??Me trae agua?? sounds natural when asking a server to bring water.

realLifeMission.title: Real Life Mission
realLifeMission.mission: Order a drink and a meal with Carlos, then ask for the bill using polite Spanish.
successCriteria: Use at least three lesson vocabulary items.
Answer one follow-up question from Carlos.

review.summary: You practiced food & drinks for everyday Spanish.
canDo: Answer Carlos with simple complete sentences.
Use the Real Life Mission in a practical situation.
selfCheck: Can I use the main lesson phrases in a complete sentence?
Can I answer Carlos with a short natural response?
Can I use one polite phrase from earlier lessons?
Can I complete the real life mission without reading every word?
